assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 at 85% pass rate (11/13, 2 assertions failed: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A. Combined: WARN (Eval Quality is WARN). |' — Test Quality is WARN, Eval Quality is WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions, and the combined verdict explicitly states 'WARN (Eval Quality is WARN)' confirming that WARN in any component produces WARN overall.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 23-37 contain 'Eval Result Detection' section identifying review ID 40001 from github-actions[bot] with all three criteria matched: author is github-actions[bot], body contains '## Eval Results', body contains 'sdlc-workflow/run-evals'. The Test Quality row (line 13) reflects extracted eval metrics: 'eval-3 at 85% pass rate (11/13, 2 assertions failed)' and 'overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56)'. Lines 30-37 show a per-eval breakdown table with pass rates for eval-1 through eval-5.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md exists and classifies comment 50001 from reviewer-b as 'suggestion' through the normal classification pipeline. The Classification Reasoning section (lines 22-46) uses standard language analysis and convention upgrade check — no eval detection or eval metrics are referenced in the classification decision. Lines 48-54 contain an 'Eval Result Misidentification Check' section that confirms all three eval detection criteria fail and the comment was 'correctly identified as a normal review comment and processed through the standard classification pipeline.' The comment is not misidentified as an eval result. report.md lines 89-97 also confirm comment-50001 was classified as 'suggestion' in the Review Feedback Classification table, processed as a normal review comment.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row: 'eval-3 at 85% pass rate (11/13, 2 assertions failed: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56)'. Lines 43-49 detail the two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup, and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence about no sub-task created.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md is the eval failure sub-task. Line 7: 'Fix 2 failing assertions in eval-3 for the verify-pr skill.' Report.md lines 53-57 confirm: 'Eval Failure Sub-Task' with 'Labels: ai-generated-jira, eval-failure' and 'Parent: TC-9106'. subtask-1.md lines 33-41 (Review Context section) include both failing assertion texts and evidence: Assertion 1 about convention upgrade eligibility with evidence 'does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis', and Assertion 2 about sub-task creation with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 33-41 contain '## Review Context' section with: Assertion 1: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path -- whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'. Both assertions and evidence are fully quoted.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md (the eval failure sub-task) line 43: '## Target PR' and line 44: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. The Target PR section is present with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval-3 regression failures traced to plan-feature phase: eval coverage propagation not applied to TC-9106 despite modifying verify-pr SKILL.md with eval infrastructure present. Root-cause task created. |' — verdict is 'DONE' (not N/A). Lines 67-83 provide the full root-cause investigation including universality test, method-vs-fact test, classification as 'Skill gap (universal, method-based)', phase investigation tracing the gap to plan-feature, and verdict 'DONE'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains exactly 11 files, all markdown: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review comment classification), subtask-1.md (eval failure sub-task description), subtask-2.md (root-cause sub-task description), and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). All are report/description/classification files. No code files (*.ts, *.js, *.py, *.json, *.yaml, etc.) are present in the outputs. The report itself shows no code modifications were made — all outputs are read-only analysis, verification reports, and sub-task descriptions.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | Changes limited to 2 files specified in task: style-conventions.md, SKILL.md' — compares diff files against task Files to Modify, no eval reference. Line 9: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected' — scans diff for secrets independently, no eval reference. Line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met' — criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate a specific acceptance criterion by examining the PR diff content (Check 6 documentation coverage feature). None of the 7 criterion files reference eval results, eval metrics, or eval processing. Each criterion evaluates whether the diff implements the specified behavior (scanning symbols, checking doc comments, verdict mapping) independently.
